channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Congress keeps kicking surveillance reform down the road
item.description: Congress has reauthorized Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act - but only for another 45 days. The extension is meant to give legislators more time to negotiate reforms to the controversial wiretapping bill. If the past few weeks are any indication of how future debates will go, however, we're in for a bumpy […]

item.title: Apple’s iPhone revenue jumps to $57 billion despite chip shortages
item.description: Apple's iPhone revenue jumped 22 percent to $57 billion over the past few months, despite supply chain issues impacting the device's processor. During an interview with Reuters, Apple CEO Tim Cook said iPhone "demand was off the charts" but added that there's "a little less flexibility in the supply chain at the moment for getting […]

item.title: Alibaba's Metis agent cuts redundant AI tool calls from 98% to 2% — and gets more accurate doing it
item.description: One of the key challenges of building effective AI agents is teaching them to choose between using external tools or relying on their internal knowledge. But large language models are often trained to blindly invoke tools, which causes latency bottlenecks, unnecessary API costs, and degraded reasoning caused by environmental noise. To overcome this challenge, researchers at Alibaba introduced Hierarchical Decoupled Policy Optimization (HDPO), a reinforcement learning framework that trains agents to balance both execution efficiency and task accuracy. Metis, a multimodal model they trained usin...

item.title: Modder releases loader to turn the PS5 into a Linux system
item.description: Linux is having a very busy year.

item.title: Prime Video will stream three Duke basketball games next season
item.description: This marks Amazon's first streaming deal with a college team.

item.title: Legal AI startup Legora hits $5.6B valuation and its battle with Harvey just got hotter
item.description: The two wildly fast-growing rivals have raised massive sums, pushed into each other's home turf and now have dueling ad campaigns.

item.title: 'Nearly two-thirds of spam came from US-based infrastructure': Your free Gmail account could be helping criminals send 46% of all commercial spam while wearing down employees with email fatigue
item.description: Attackers exploit trusted email platforms, user fatigue, and legitimate infrastructure to bypass defenses, making phishing attacks more effective and harder to detect.

item.title: Rivian’s revenue is up as R2 production kicks into gear
item.description: Rivian reported its first quarter earnings of 2026, providing us a closer look at the company's financial health as it kicks off production for the crucial R2 electric vehicle. We've already got Rivian's production and delivery statement from the first three months of the year. The company sold 10,365 vehicles in Q1, representing 20 percent […]

item.title: Rivian downsizes new EV factory after Trump’s DOE slashes loan agreement
item.description: Rivian announced some changes today with regard to the factory its building in the state of Georgia. The company was planning to build the facility in two phases, each resulting in 200,000 vehicles of annual production capacity, for a total of 400,000 units. Rivian held a ground breaking ceremony late last year. Now the company […]

item.title: Rivian downsizes DOE loan to $4.5B, while boosting capacity of Georgia factory
item.description: Rivian has reworked its loan deal with the Department of Energy and now expects to borrow $4.5 billion to build its new factory in Georgia, down from the original amount of $6.6 billion.

item.title: Clever Raspberry Pi hack gets Sony's PS5 controller working properly on a gaming PC over Bluetooth
item.description: This Raspberry Pi mod is a cheap and effective way to get Sony's DualSense controller working properly on a PC with a wireless connection.

item.title: Employers are blindsiding candidates with AI interviews—and scaring them off
item.description: A report from hiring platform Greenhouse found that 63% of job seekers have been interviewed by AI during the hiring process—and they’re not happy about it. It’s no secret that artificial intelligence has penetrated every aspect of the hiring process—even the elements that should necessitate a human touch, like conducting interviews. The vast majority of companies already rely on AI to sift through applications and resumes, but many of them are now also using it for screening calls and initial interviews. ...

item.title: The logic of the racist Supreme Court isn’t adding up
item.description: Close watchers of the Supreme Court knew that the conservative supermajority was about to murder what was left of the Voting Rights Act. Wednesday's decision in Louisiana v. Callais took down Section 2 of the law, clearing the way for racist gerrymandering, because it is now racist to remedy racism. The decision is an affront […]

item.title: You can now choose which games use Quick Resume on Xbox
item.description: The option is part of a collection of updates Microsoft is making to consoles and the Xbox PC app.

item.title: No 401(k) plan? You could have a new option to save for retirement soon
item.description: President Trump on Thursday signed an executive order aimed at expanding access to retirement plans for workers whose employers don’t provide one. An opportunity for more Americans to save for their retirement may be on the way.

item.title: Hackers are actively exploiting a bug in cPanel, used by millions of websites
item.description: Web hosts are scrambling to fix the bug under active attack by hackers. One company said hackers have been abusing the bug for months.

item.title: Has smart home tech gone too far? This robot vacuum is also a closet — and four washer-dryers
item.description: This Dreame robot vacuum comes with four washer dryers — and a closet

item.title: Chipotle just shocked Wall Street and it could change what you’re paying for lunch
item.description: Same-store sales flipped positive when analysts expected a drop and it signals a turnaround after a rough year Chipotle has been tweaking its recipe to lure diners back, and in the first quarter of the year it appears to be working.

item.title: After dissing Anthropic for limiting Mythos, OpenAI restricts access to Cyber, too
item.description: OpenAI will begin rolling out its cybersecurity testing tool, GPT-5.5 Cyber only "to critical cyber defenders" at first.

item.title: We Go Up Close With Ultimate Grogu: Hasbro’s $600 Star Wars Animatronic
item.description: Hasbro reveals Ultimate Grogu, a $600 Star Wars animatronic collectible. Here’s a first look at the prototype and preorder details.

item.title: Microsoft’s Xbox mode is now available for all Windows 11 PCs
item.description: Microsoft is now rolling out its Xbox mode to all Windows 11 PCs. The new Xbox mode adds a full-screen interface to the Xbox PC app, much like Steam's Big Picture Mode, and originally debuted as the Xbox Full Screen Experience (FSE) with Asus' Xbox Ally devices. "Some players in select markets will be able […]
